title: CSS Borders, Shadows, and Outlines Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential properties for adding depth and definition to your web elements using CSS.

questions.question: Which CSS property is specifically used to round the corners of an element?
What is the primary difference between a border and an outline in CSS?
options: A border takes up space in the layout, while an outline does not.
An outline is only for images, and a border is only for text.
A border is always black, and an outline is always white.

questions.question: Which property is used to add a shadow behind an element to create a three-dimensional effect (common in cards)?
Which text-shadow value determines how 'spread out' or 'soft' the shadow appears?
The 'outline' property is primarily used for what important purpose?
options: To make the site load much faster.
For accessibility (showing which element has focus).
To satisfy search engine crawler requirements.
